title: The Value of Volunteering
description: For questions 17 – 24, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Thousands of people around the world give their time on a (0) voluntary basis, helping others without expecting payment. The (17) beneficial effects of volunteering are well documented and include improved mental health and greater social connection. Volunteers often work within local communities, assisting with activities that may otherwise go unfunded. Running a successful volunteer programme requires careful (18) organisation and strong leadership skills. Most volunteers report a deep sense of personal (19) satisfaction from knowing that their efforts have made a positive difference. The skills gained through volunteering are (20) valuable and are increasingly recognised by employers. Some volunteers are (21) unemployed and use their experience to develop new abilities and improve career prospects. Charities rely on (22) supportive networks of volunteers to deliver services that would otherwise be unaffordable. Taking on a volunteer role requires personal (23) responsibility and a genuine (24) commitment to the cause.

example.text: Thousands of people around the world give their time on a (0) VOLUNTARY basis, helping others without expecting payment. (VOLUNTEER)

questions.explanation: An adjective is needed before 'effects'; 'benefit' becomes 'beneficial' by adding the suffix '-ial'.
A noun is needed after 'careful'; 'organise' becomes 'organisation' by replacing '-ise' with '-isation'.
A noun is needed after 'sense of personal'; 'satisfy' becomes 'satisfaction' by changing '-fy' to '-faction'.
An adjective is needed after the verb 'are'; 'value' becomes 'valuable' by adding the suffix '-able'.
A negative adjective is needed; 'employ' → 'employed', then the prefix 'un-' creates 'unemployed', meaning 'without a job'.
An adjective is needed before 'networks'; 'support' becomes 'supportive' by adding the suffix '-ive'.
A noun is needed after 'personal'; 'responsible' becomes 'responsibility' by changing '-ble' to '-bility'.
A noun is needed after 'genuine'; 'commit' becomes 'commitment' by adding the suffix '-ment'.
